text.title: Rust Project Structure (Axum/Actix) Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real rust-project-structure deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: main.rs grows to 400 lines: config parsing + DB pool + every handler wired inline.
antipattern.fix: main.rs ≤80 lines: config + state + router + serve; everything else in submodules.

antipattern.symptom: src/lib.rs contains handlers, services, DB, models — one file 2000+ lines.
Adding a new entity touches 5 files inconsistently; some PRs miss the service layer.
antipattern.root-cause: Onboarding doc instead of a scaffold script.
antipattern.fix: new_module.sh creates model+db+service+handler+route stubs in one command.
